contexts.title: Daily habits
contexts.description: Routines at home, school, and personal life.

contexts.title: IT work
contexts.description: Software, support, meetings, and technical work.

contexts.title: Family routines
contexts.description: Daily activities with family and children.

contexts.title: Travel and vacation
contexts.description: Trips, plans, hotels, and free time.

contexts.title: Prepositions
contexts.description: Place, time, and movement words used in examples.

units.title: Present Simple Foundation
units.focus: Use Present Simple for habits, routines, facts, and schedules.
objectives: Recognize when Present Simple is the right tense.
Build affirmative, negative, and question forms.

sections.title: Meaning
body: Present Simple describes habits, routines, general truths, permanent situations, and schedules.
It usually answers the question: what do you usually do?

structures.pattern: Subject + base verb (+ s/es for he/she/it)
structures.example: He works as an IT engineer.
He does not work on Sundays.

mistakes.why: He/she/it needs -s in affirmative Present Simple.

mistakes.wrong: He does not works on Sundays.
mistakes.right: He does not work on Sundays.
mistakes.why: Does already carries the third-person marker, so the main verb returns to base form.

examples.sentence: Dario checks his email before he starts coding.
examples.note: This describes a regular work routine.

examples.sentence: He drops off his kids at school every morning.
examples.note: Every morning signals a repeated habit.

examples.sentence: She joins the planning meeting every Tuesday.
examples.note: Every Tuesday shows a regular schedule.

examples.sentence: They cook dinner together on Fridays.
examples.note: On Fridays points to a repeated family routine.

examples.sentence: We book a hotel before every vacation.
examples.note: Before every vacation describes a repeated travel habit.

examples.sentence: The laptop stays on the desk during the day.
examples.note: On the desk uses a place preposition in a simple fact.

vocabulary.example: The team deploys the app on Friday.
They have a stand-up every morning.
He picks up his kids after school.

vocabulary.meaning: to put things in a bag before a trip
vocabulary.example: We pack our bags before vacation.

vocabulary.meaning: a word for something that happens most of the time
a preposition for days and surfaces
vocabulary.example: The report is on the desk.

exercises.explanation: Dario is he, so the verb work becomes works.

exercises.prompt: Change to a question: He works in IT.
exercises.explanation: Use does + subject + base verb for he/she/it questions.

exercises.prompt: Choose the tense: Dario checks his email every morning.
exercises.explanation: Every morning signals a repeated routine, so Simple Present is the best tense.

exercises.prompt: She _____ the planning meeting every Tuesday.
exercises.explanation: She needs the -s form in affirmative Present Simple.

exercises.prompt: He _____ up his kids after school.
exercises.explanation: He needs the -s form: picks up.
